description: US special education rights for children with disabilities: IDEA, Section 504, IEP process, manifestation determination, dispute resolution. Federal floor — state rules may add protections.
source: IDEA 2004 (20 U.S.C. § 1400 et seq.), Section 504 Rehabilitation Act 1973 (29 U.S.C. § 794), 34 CFR Part 300, OSEP guidance

triples.subject: IDEA
triples.object: Individuals with Disabilities Education Act — federal law guaranteeing free appropriate public education (FAPE) for children with disabilities

triples.subject: IDEA
triples.object: 20 U.S.C. § 1400 et seq.; implementing regulations at 34 CFR Part 300

triples.subject: IDEA
triples.object: covers children ages 3–21 (or through graduation); Part C covers birth through age 2 (Early Intervention)

triples.subject: FAPE
triples.object: Free Appropriate Public Education — special education and related services at no cost, designed to meet the child's unique needs

triples.subject: IDEA eligibility
triples.object: 13 disability categories: autism, deaf-blindness, deafness, emotional disturbance, hearing impairment, intellectual disability, multiple disabilities, orthopedic impairment, other health impairment, SLD, speech/language, TBI, visual impairment

triples.subject: IDEA evaluation
triples.object: parents can request evaluation in writing; school must evaluate within 60 days (or state timeline) at no cost to family

triples.subject: IDEA evaluation
triples.object: if parents disagree with school evaluation, they may request an Independent Educational Evaluation (IEE) at public expense

triples.subject: IEP
triples.object: Individualized Education Program — written plan describing services, goals, accommodations for each eligible child

triples.subject: IEP
triples.object: parents, regular education teacher, special education teacher, school representative, child (if appropriate), evaluation interpreter

triples.subject: IEP
triples.object: present levels of performance, annual goals, special education services, participation in general education, accommodations, transition planning (age 16+)

triples.subject: IEP meeting
triples.object: must be reviewed at least annually; parents can request a meeting at any time

triples.subject: IEP meeting
triples.object: prior written notice before any change; right to bring advocate or attorney; right to disagree and dispute

triples.subject: prior written notice
triples.object: school must provide written notice whenever it proposes to or refuses to change identification, evaluation, placement, or services

triples.subject: Section 504
triples.object: Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, 29 U.S.C. § 794 — bars disability discrimination by federally funded entities

triples.subject: Section 504
triples.object: covers any disability substantially limiting a major life activity — does not require educational impact for eligibility

triples.subject: Section 504 plan
triples.object: accommodations and modifications in general education: extended time, preferential seating, copy of notes, sensory breaks

triples.subject: Section 504 vs IDEA
triples.object: IDEA provides specialized instruction and services with enforceable IEP; 504 provides accommodations only, no specialized instruction

triples.subject: LRE
triples.object: Least Restrictive Environment — IDEA requires children be educated alongside non-disabled peers to the maximum extent appropriate

triples.subject: manifestation determination
triples.object: required review when school proposes to suspend IDEA-eligible child for 10+ days or change placement due to behavior

triples.subject: manifestation determination
triples.object: was behavior caused by or substantially related to child's disability? Was it direct result of school's failure to implement IEP?

triples.subject: manifestation determination
triples.object: child must return to original placement (unless safety exception); IEP team must conduct FBA and revise behavior plan

triples.subject: FBA
triples.object: Functional Behavioral Assessment — evaluation to determine function/reason for behavior and develop appropriate interventions

triples.subject: dispute resolution IDEA
triples.object: State Complaint to State Education Agency, mediation, due process hearing, federal court — multiple tracks available

triples.subject: State Complaint
triples.object: SEA must investigate and issue written decision within 60 days; must be filed within 1 year of alleged violation

triples.subject: due process
triples.object: request must be filed within 2 years of violation; hearing officer issues decision within 45 days of end of resolution period

triples.subject: parent surrogate
triples.object: when child has no parent or parent cannot be located, school must assign surrogate parent to protect IDEA rights

triples.subject: child find
triples.object: schools are affirmatively required to identify and evaluate all children with suspected disabilities including homeless, private school, migrant

triples.subject: Section 504 complaint
triples.object: US Department of Education Office for Civil Rights (OCR) within 180 days of discrimination; ed.gov/ocr

triples.subject: special education advocate
triples.object: trained non-attorney who assists families at IEP meetings and in disputes; not a lawyer but often effective and lower cost

triples.subject: Parent Training and Information Center
triples.object: free information, training, and assistance to families of children with disabilities — one in every state

triples.subject: Parent Training and Information Center
triples.object: parentcenterhub.org — federally funded, staffed by parents of children with disabilities
